steps.title: Convert the first percentage
steps.content: Particle A's speed is $6{,}000\%$ of particle C's speed: $$a = \frac{6000}{100} \cdot c = 60c.$$

steps.title: Convert the second percentage
steps.content: Particle C's speed is $0.008\%$ of particle B's speed: $$c = \frac{0.008}{100} \cdot b = 0.00008\,b.$$ Solving for $b$ gives $$b = \frac{c}{0.00008} = 12{,}500\,c.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $12{,}560c$ matches the derived sum. Keep.
  • B: $6{,}125c$ would require $b \approx 6{,}065c$, far from $12{,}500c$. Eliminate.
  • C: $6{,}008c$ is roughly $a$ plus $\frac{1}{2}b$ only; too small. Eliminate.
  • D: $1{,}850c$ is far below the actual sum. Eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
